symptoms.symptom_name: Acute panic and racing heart triggered by viewing a low platelet result online

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A discrete panic episode with a clear cognitive trigger is characteristic of health-anxiety-driven panic.
Bilateral, band-like, pressing quality with gradual onset is classic for tension-type headache.
A gradual tension-pattern headache is not the sudden thunderclap pattern of intracranial haemorrhage.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpitations, hyperventilation and sense of impending doom

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Autonomic arousal with a sense of impending doom is core to a panic attack.
Reassurance-seeking and compulsive checking are hallmarks of health anxiety.

symptoms.symptom_name: No focal neurological deficit on history (no weakness, speech, visual change or vomiting)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of focal deficit and vomiting makes a significant intracranial bleed much less likely, though telehealth cannot fully exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: No active bleeding (denies epistaxis, gum bleeding, haematuria, melaena, haematemesis)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of any mucosal or systemic bleeding argues strongly against a clinically dangerous low count.
Bleeding screen is required to move critical thrombocytopenia off the differential.

symptoms.symptom_name: No new petechiae, purpura or bruising on patient self-inspection

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of wet purpura/petechiae on self-inspection reduces (but, being patient-reported, does not definitively exclude) impending major bleeding.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Trend and severity of thrombocytopenia cannot be assessed

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Isolated mild thrombocytopenia with otherwise normal counts fits immune thrombocytopenia.
At ~75 x10^9/L the count is far above the <10-20 range where spontaneous bleeding occurs.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dermatological examination clear — no petechiae, purpura or ecchymoses anywhere

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A definitively clear skin examination excludes wet purpura — but this is a clinician finding, sealed and unavailable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Oropharynx clear — no haemorrhagic bullae or gingival bleeding

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of oral wet purpura on examination is reassuring against impending major bleeding; clinician-only finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal neurological examination (cranial nerves, power, sensation, gait intact)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal formal neurological exam strongly argues against an intracranial bleed — but the AI Doctor cannot perform it and must rely on history.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sinus tachycardia (HR ~110) and mild hypertension (BP 145/90) on measurement

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sympathetically-driven sinus tachycardia and transient hypertension fit acute panic; non-specific and reproducible via patient-worn devices.
Tachycardia is non-specific and does not by itself confirm the diagnosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No bone pain, night sweats, weight loss, recurrent infection or hepatosplenomegaly

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of systemic B-symptoms, organomegaly and additional cytopenias argues against marrow failure / leukaemia.
Absence of blood-thinning medication removes an additional bleeding-risk multiplier.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute anxiety / panic attack with health anxiety
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Discrete panic episode with a clear cognitive trigger and autonomic arousal — the leading explanation for the presentation.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Absent bleeding and neurological red flags
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Exclusion of bleeding emergency / intracranial haemorrhage
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of negative bleeding and neurological screening is what makes a low-tier disposition safe; note the physical-sign members are clinician-only and unavailable in telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Repeat full blood count with platelet count
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the count and trend; arranged through the GP, not urgently.
Part of the standard work-up of thrombocytopenia (excludes pseudothrombocytopenia and blasts); GP/haematology directed.
There are no neurological red flags; imaging here would be low-value, would reinforce health anxiety, and is only indicated if focal neurology or thunderclap headache develops.
Reasonable baseline as part of GP work-up; typically normal in immune thrombocytopenia.
